name: Proanthocyanidins
summary: Proanthocyanidins is a polyphenols associated with cranberry. Evidence level: moderate.
description: Proanthocyanidins is cataloged as a polyphenols. Reported mechanism notes: Primary inhibition of bacterial adhesion (human-supported via extracts); additional mechanisms (preclinical).
mechanisms: Primary inhibition of bacterial adhesion (human-supported via extracts)
raw_mechanisms: Primary inhibition of bacterial adhesion (human-supported via extracts)
unmapped_mechanisms: Primary inhibition of bacterial adhesion (human-supported via extracts)
conditions: not enough human evidence for a consumer recommendation
buying_criteria: Do not promote as a general consumer product
keep as research/reference unless human supplement evidence and safety context are added
